positional_bias.note: Derived distribution matches sidecar exactly — no meta_gift_desync.

length_bias.note: q1 correct answer 115 chars vs distractor mean 84.3 (1.36x); q3 correct answer 101 chars vs distractor mean 55.3 (1.83x). Both flagged individually but aggregate rate 0.333 is below the 0.40 hard-fail threshold.

weakest_questions.distractor: The user prompt is called live because the user types it in real time, while the system prompt is pre-recorded audio played to the model.
weakest_questions.note: Pre-recorded audio framing is implausible for any text-API context. Weak but does not breach 0.25 threshold.

soft_findings: q2 has one weak distractor (pre-recorded audio, score=1) that is borderline absurd but does not breach the 0.25 absurd_rate threshold.

q1 and q3 have a length imbalance where the correct answer is noticeably longer than distractors; aggregate rate (0.333) stays below the 0.40 hard-fail threshold but author should consider tightening those answers in a future revision.
verdict: Pass — all positional-bias, length-bias, distractor-plausibility, and LO-coverage checks clean. Two soft findings logged (q2 weak distractor; q1/q3 length skew) but neither crosses a hard-fail threshold.
